concerns.text: Task T029e (Create templates.py) is listed AFTER T029a (generate_bio_candidates.py) which explicitly depends on it ('Use code/screening/templates.py (created in T029e)'). This violates the producer-before-consumer rule. T029e must precede T029a.

Task T044 (Add high variance check to validate_dataset.py) is placed in Phase 7 (Revision), but the logic it describes (excluding high variance entries) is a prerequisite for the training data used in Phase 4 (US2). T015 already flags these entries, but T044 implies the exclusion logic is missing until Phase 7. If T015 only flags and T044 excludes, the model in T021 trains on dirty data. The exclusion logic must be in Phase 3 (US1) before T016 (standardized output) or T021 (training).

Task T029c (load_experimental_benchmarks.py) depends on T029b (generate_petro_benchmarks.py) to provide the SMILES, but T029c is listed before T029b in the task list. While T029c description mentions T029b, the ordering in the list implies T029c runs first. T029b must precede T029c.

Task T014b (curate_known_bio_membranes.py) is listed after T014 (validate_dataset.py). T014 description explicitly states it must 'Verify >= 10 known high-performance bio-membranes exist by checking the aggregated dataset'. If T014b creates the file T014 checks, T014b must precede T014. Currently, T014 checks a file that doesn't exist yet in the order.
